title: Extension re-expands: a source-control-style Hunch Memory view (timeline of memory moves)
context: The auto-trust model makes memory land + auto-commit seamlessly in the background; the user needs a way to SEE and UNDO what Hunch does without managing a queue. The prior cut (dec_a7f5ac92cb) reduced the extension to Why + Capture and removed all panels — that is now superseded on purpose.
decision: The VS Code extension gains a 'Hunch Memory' activity-bar view: a source-control-style timeline of every memory MOVE (capture/adopt/supersede/prune), read from `hunch log --json` (the CLI owns git; the extension stays a pure reader). Each move is click-to-diff (`hunch log --diff <sha>` rendered as a read-only diff popup) and right-click revert (`hunch revert-move <sha>` = local git revert, never pushed). Title actions: Sync now, Adopt drafts, Approve-to-push (the one explicit outward step — auto-commit stays LOCAL by design). New CLI surface: `hunch log`, `hunch revert-move`, `hunch push`; new module src/core/memorylog.ts (pure parser) + extractors/git helpers gitMemoryLog/memoryMoveDiff/revertMemoryMove/pushCurrentBranch. Extension v0.15.0.
consequences: The extension is no longer strictly two surfaces — the one-sentence litmus becomes: memory you can see and undo.
Auto-commit is ON by default (local); pushing is a deliberate human click (approve-to-push).
Memory moves are individually revertable, so autonomous capture is safe to leave running.
alternatives_rejected: Keep the 2-surface cut and put the timeline in the CLI/wiki only (rejected: the user explicitly wants a source-control panel on the side)
Auto-push memory to GitHub in the background (rejected: publishing is outward + hard to undo; approve-to-push keeps the human gate)
Let the extension run git directly (rejected: the extension stays a pure reader; all git/writes go through the hunch CLI)
